question: Who was on-call for DC-A at 2025-03-15 14:30 UTC?
What is the commit hash of the deployment to DC-A api-gateway at 2025-03-15 14:22 UTC?

How many P0 incidents occurred in DC-A in Q1 2025 (January–March)?
What was the CPU% on dc-a-host-02 at 2025-03-15 14:40 UTC?
Which runbook handles DC-B payment-service memory exhaustion?
What is the commit hash of the rollback deployed to DC-A on 2025-03-15 15:52 UTC?
Who was on-call for DC-C at 2024-11-20 03:00 UTC?
Which service had the most P0 incidents across all datacenters over the full 18-month period?
How many deployments were made to DC-B payment-service in September 2024?
What was the total number of P0 incidents across all datacenters in 2024?
What was the mean MTTR (in minutes) for P0 incidents in DC-A over the full dataset?
Which datacenter had the fewest P0 incidents in H2 2024, and how many?
How many deployments occurred across all datacenters and services in March 2025?
What was the average CPU% across all DC-A api-gateway hosts during 2025-03-15 14:25–15:10 UTC?
How many P0 incidents did auth-service experience in all of 2024?
How many P1 incidents were opened within 30 minutes of any deployment, across the full dataset?
What was the mean memory% on DC-B payment-service hosts in the 4 hours following deploy d-2024090301?
How many 5-minute intervals showed network throughput above the 95th percentile on DC-C data-pipeline hosts in November 2024?
How many incidents (any severity) occurred in the 24 hours before deploy d-2025031501?
Did deploy d-2025031501 on 2025-03-15 cause P0 incidents in DC-A? How many, within what timeframe, and what are their IDs?
answer: Yes. Deploy d-2025031501 (2025-03-15 14:22 UTC) directly caused 4 P0 incidents (INC-4821, INC-4822, INC-4823, INC-4824) in DC-A api-gateway, all opened within 13 minutes of the deployment.

question: Is there a temporal correlation between the CPU spike on DC-A api-gateway hosts and the P0 incident cluster on 2025-03-15?
answer: Yes. CPU on dc-a-host-01 through dc-a-host-04 began rising at 14:25 and peaked at 14:38 (avg 87.4%). All 4 P0 incidents were opened between 14:28 and 14:35 — during the rising phase. Pearson correlation between avg CPU% and active P0 count in 5-min buckets during 14:25–15:10 is > 0.85.

question: Did the rollback deploy d-2025031502 at 15:52 UTC cause CPU to recover on DC-A api-gateway hosts?
answer: Yes. Within 8 minutes of the rollback deploy at 15:52, CPU on all 4 affected DC-A api-gateway hosts returned below 60%.

question: How did deploy d-2024090301 affect DC-B payment-service memory over the 4 hours following deployment?
answer: Memory on dc-b-host-06 and dc-b-host-07 increased monotonically at approximately 7.5%/hour from a 55% baseline, reaching ~85% after 4 hours. Mean memory over the 4-hour window was 79.3%.

question: Was the DC-C data-pipeline network storm on 2024-11-20 preceded by a deployment?
answer: No. There were no deployments to DC-C data-pipeline in the 6 hours preceding the network storm that started at 03:00 UTC. The cause was a batch job misconfiguration, not a code deploy.

question: Who handled the 2025-03-15 P0 incidents in DC-A, and when were they all resolved?
answer: Alice Johnson (on-call for DC-A, shift 08:00–20:00 UTC) was assigned all 4 P0 incidents (INC-4821 through INC-4824). All were resolved at 16:45 UTC, giving an MTTR range of 130–137 minutes.

question: Across the full 18-month dataset, which deployments caused a P0 incident within 15 minutes of being deployed?
Did cache-service experience any incidents that were temporally correlated with deployments in DC-A over the 18-month period?
answer: No. cache-service in DC-A had no incidents opened within 30 minutes of any deployment throughout the full dataset.

question: After runbook RB-payment-002 was applied to the 2024-09-03 DC-B payment-service incidents, how quickly did memory recover?
answer: Memory on the affected hosts (dc-b-host-06, dc-b-host-07) returned below 70% within 45 minutes of the incident being resolved at 13:30 UTC via RB-payment-002.

question: On 2025-03-15, did all four DC-A api-gateway hosts show elevated CPU simultaneously, or was it staggered?
answer: Simultaneous. All four hosts (dc-a-host-01 through dc-a-host-04) exceeded 80% CPU by 14:30 UTC — within 5 minutes of the first spike at 14:25 — confirming a shared-cause outage rather than a cascading failure.
